name: Warden
flavor: The protective discipline of the wire: a Puppeteer who strings up allies not to move them like pieces on a board, but to hold a line back to them, ready to snap taut the instant one is struck.

features.name: Warding Pull
features.description: When a creature you have a String attached to is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to snap its wire taut, guiding it half a step aside at the last instant and granting it resistance to the damage from that attack. This draws on the same reaction as Parry, Disrupting Pull, and Guiding Pull — you still get only one reaction per round, so protecting an ally this way costs the same reaction you'd otherwise spend defending yourself or disrupting a foe.

features.name: Steadfast Line
features.description: A willing creature you have a String attached to has advantage on saving throws made to avoid being knocked prone or forcibly moved, as long as it remains within your leash range — your wire keeps it steady on its feet. Additionally, if such a creature is knocked prone anyway, it can spend only 5 feet of movement (instead of half its speed) to stand back up, guided upright by the string.

features.name: Keeper's Thread
features.description: Some strings aren't for pulling — they're for holding on. Over 1 minute, you can tie a hair-fine thread to a willing creature you touch. For the next 24 hours, while the creature is within 1 mile of you, you know its direction and its general condition (unharmed, wounded, unconscious, or dead), and if it is in danger while you sleep, the thread's tug wakes you instantly. You can maintain a number of threads equal to your {{Charisma modifier (min 1)|set from your stats on your character sheet}}.
features.sheetSummary: 1-min ritual, touch, willing creature: for 24 h know its direction + condition (unharmed/wounded/unconscious/dead) within 1 mile; its danger wakes you from sleep. Cha mod threads at once (min 1).
